violations.text: Spinach with smoked turkey was observed held at 103 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
Various items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Inspector observed cutting boards being stored upright on the floor between 1 door fridge and freezer chest. PIC placed in 3 bay sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen (green and red) are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Wood is used in the construction of veggie slicer (fried plantain chips). Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Inspector suggested plastic or metal slicer. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - hot-holding unit - 1 door freezer units (both) New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Chicken and fish was observed held at (112-118) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated foods to proper temperature during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The presence of (ants) is not controlled as evidenced by (live activity). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the (pots, fryer, hot hold unit) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
Cold water knob on 3 bay sink observed in poor repair(missing). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The threshold to the skylight in kitchen is in poor repair. Inspector observed exposed wood and drywall The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The presence of ants is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fryer, cooking pans and hot hold unit have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
3 bay sink plumbing was observed in poor repair. Water observed leaking onto floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
Paint in some areas of the kitchen (walls and ceiling) are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
The floor in the kitchen area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024
